key_insight: Style-transfer-based unsupervised domain adaptation plus a Swin Transformer V2 classifier improved calcification malignancy classification across mammography sites with different vendors and image types.
clinical_relevance: This approach could make mammography AI more reliable when deployed at new hospitals or on different imaging systems, helping reduce performance drops from dataset shift.
method: Unsupervised domain adaptation using AdaIN and CycleGAN to create vendor- and technique-specific training samples without extra labels
Supervised calcification classification with Swin Transformer V2 as the backbone
Tested across multi-site mammography datasets including full-field digital mammography and synthetic 2D DBT-derived images
evidence_signal: Evaluated on OPTIMAM cross-validation (n=2994) with external validation on EMBED (n=125) and Duke Calcification Dataset v1 (n=788), improving AUC from 0.68 to 0.72 on EMBED and from 0.68 to 0.73 on Duke.
watch_item: Performance gains were modest, so real-world impact may still depend on robust external validation, careful calibration, and checking that generated images do not introduce artifacts or bias.
paper_title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Calcification Classification in Mammography Across Multi-Site Datasets
